title: vigna membranacea subsp. caesia
intro: Vigna membranacea subsp. caesia, also known as the hairy bean or hairy vetch, is a perennial legume native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vigna membranacea subsp. caesia include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or extract, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
